If your garage door won't open and you need someone **today in Dublin, CA** , these companies advertise same-day or emergency service in the area:

  * Silver Door Garage Door Specialists — 24/7 service, same-day repairs for broken springs, cables, openers, and doors that won't open. Phone: (925) 578-2873. [24/7 Garage Door Repair in Dublin, CA | Silver Door Garage Door Specialists](https://silverdoorgaragedoordublin.com/?utm_source=chatgpt.com)[Garage Door Repair in Pleasanton, CA | Silver Door Garage Door Specialists](https://silverdoorgaragedoordublin.com/service-areas/pleasanton?utm_source=chatgpt.com)

  * Dully Garage Door Repair Inc. — Advertises 24/7 availability and same-day service. Phone: (510) 828-6112. [Contact – Dully Garage Door Repair Inc.](https://dullygaragedoorusa.com/contact/?utm_source=chatgpt.com)[Garage Door Remote Control Repair and Replacement – Dully Garage Door Repair Inc.](https://dullygaragedoorusa.com/service/garage-door-remote-control-repair-and-replacement/?utm_source=chatgpt.com)[Broken Spring Repair and Replacement – Dully Garage Door Repair Inc.](https://dullygaragedoorusa.com/service/broken-spring-repair-and-replacement/?utm_source=chatgpt.com)

  * Integrity Garage Doors & Gates — Serves Dublin and the Tri-Valley with same-day repair availability. [Bay Area Garage Door & Gate Repair | 24/7 Same-Day Service](https://integritygd.com/?utm_source=chatgpt.com)[Garage Door & Gate Services Dublin CA | Tri-Valley](https://integritygd.com/locations/dublin?utm_source=chatgpt.com)

  * GoPro Garage Door Repair — Offers same- or next-day service and repairs for springs, openers, tracks, and motors. Phone: (888) 987-0933. [Dublin Garage Door Repair & Installation | GoPro Dublin, CA](https://www.goprogaragedoorrepair.com/dublin/?utm_source=chatgpt.com)

  * Graham Garage Doors — Advertises same-day service calls and 24/7 emergency assistance. Bay Area phone: (925) 344-1274. [Garage Door Repair in Dublin, CA | Graham Garage Doors](https://www.grahamgaragedoors.net/garage-door-repair-in-dublin-ca?utm_source=chatgpt.com)

Before calling, a quick safety check:

  * If you hear the opener running but the door doesn't move, a broken spring is possible.

  * If the door is crooked or hanging on one side, a cable may have failed.

  * Don't try to force the door open if a spring or cable is broken; those components are under high tension.

Can you tell me:

  1. Is the door completely closed, partially open, or fully open?

  2. Do you hear the opener motor when you press the remote?

  3. Did you hear a loud bang from the garage recently?

Those symptoms can help identify whether it's likely a spring, opener, sensor, or cable issue before you call.
